title: Hemigraphis reptans
intro: Hemigraphis reptans, commonly known as Indian turnover or purple waffle, is a low-growing, spreading plant native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Hemigraphis reptans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use in modern medicine is still largely anecdotal and requires further scientific research for confirmation.
